name: foreground-subagent
description: Parent claude session uses the Task (Agent) tool to launch one foreground subagent. The subagent reads a small file and reports a result; the parent waits for the subagent to finish, then replies 'done'. Verifies the parent's ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc wraps the subagent's lifetime, the child sidechain transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/, and the daemon emits parent_linked binding child‚Üíparent. Predecessor recipe is the orchestrator-side subagent-spawn (3 parallel children); this scenario locks down the minimal 1-child shape per the agent-level coverage matrix.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code has a first-class Task tool (a.k.a. Agent tool) that spawns sub-agents for parallel exploration. Each subagent writes an `isSidechain` transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl`. The parent waits for the subagent's completion signal ‚Äî a `task-notification` user-event in the parent transcript carrying the subagent's task-id and status ‚Äî before continuing its turn.

irrlicht's claudecode adapter observes the full topology via file-based discovery, not in-process counting:

1. The fswatcher walks `~/.claude/projects/` (transcriptsDir(), agent.go:36) and picks up every subagent transcript as its own `SessionState`.
2. The detector emits a `KindParentLinked` lifecycle event when the child session's `ParentSessionID` is set (session_detector_activity.go:110). The orchestrator-side scenario `subagent-spawn` already verifies this with `parent_linked_min: 2`.
3. `CountOpenSubagents` (parser.go:553) deliberately returns 0 because the file-based path is the single source of truth ‚Äî counting in-process Agent tool_use entries as well would double-count. The metrics layer derives `SubagentCount(parent)` from the live children that link to it.
4. Parent state: the parent's outstanding Agent tool_use prevents `IsAgentDone()` from firing, so the parent stays `working` until the tool_result for the Task arrives. `handleTaskNotification` (parser.go:192) records the subagent completion signals from the parent transcript.

Mapped to spec bullets:
- 'Parent ready ‚Üí working at spawn' ‚Äî parent's Agent tool_use line drives the transition.
- 'Child session appears with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id' ‚Äî fswatcher discovers the sidechain file and links via lifecycle KindParentLinked.
- 'SubagentCount(parent) = N while any child alive' ‚Äî derived from live linked children.
- 'Parent stays working until child ends' ‚Äî parent's pending Agent tool_use blocks IsAgentDone.
- 'Parent ‚Üí ready after last child ends' ‚Äî task-notification arrives, parent emits end_turn, working‚Üíready.

The AskUserQuestion-from-child variant works because the child has its own independent transcript and tailer state, so its `working‚Üíwaiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready` arc is computed exactly like a parent's. The orphan-on-death variant is covered by the sweep ‚Äî if the child process dies without an end-of-turn line, it's reaped by the same mechanism used in `subagent-orphan-cleanup` (assessed separately).
caveats: claudecode's subagent transcripts live under `<parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` ‚Äî a path that the curator's fixture bundler treats specially (`<fixture>.subagents/`). The orchestrator-side `subagent-spawn` recipe already validates the end-to-end shape; the agent-level coverage_id maps to `foreground-subagent` directly.

The N=3 parallel variant works because the file-based path scales linearly ‚Äî each sidechain transcript is an independent SessionState. No special-case parser logic is needed for the 1-vs-3 distinction.

sources.note: CountOpenSubagents (line 553) returns 0 by design ‚Äî file-based sidechain transcripts are the single source of truth, avoiding double-counting against open Agent tool_use entries.
lifecycle.KindParentLinked emitted at line 110 when a child SessionState's ParentSessionID is bound ‚Äî this is what the orchestrator-side verify asserts.
subagent-spawn recipe (coverage_id: foreground-subagent) ‚Äî prompts 3 parallel Explore subagents and asserts parent_linked_min: 2, subagent_transcripts_min: 2.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
claude session can spawn at least 1 subagent (default model has Task/Agent tool exposed)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude's trust dialog appears once and the driver auto-accepts)
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic file to read

script.text: Use the Task tool to launch a general-purpose subagent that reads the README.md in the current directory and reports the first heading. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent UUID entering ready, then a single ready‚Üíworking transition driven by the user prompt
events.jsonl contains at least 1 parent_linked event pointing at the parent UUID ‚Äî the child sidechain transcript was discovered and bound
transcript.jsonl contains a tool_use block with name=Agent (the Task tool invocation)
the subagent transcript is present under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl
no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent UUID between parent_linked and the final working‚Üíready (parent stays working until the child reports back)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working‚Üíready transition for the parent UUID at turn end
final session state for the parent is ready

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

Reassessment from scratch: the GREEN rollup claim (yes/full/ready) was unbacked ‚Äî `replaydata/agents/opencode/scenarios/foreground-subagent/` had no assessment.json and no recording on disk (a prior assessment was retired to `replaydata/agents/opencode/regression/foreground-subagent/`). This artifact re-derives the verdict from agent docs + source and the irrlicht adapter transport, and ratifies the GREEN claim.

## agent_supports: yes

OpenCode ships a first-class foreground-subagent primitive. A primary agent invokes the **Task tool**; the tool's `execute` calls `Session.create(ctx.sessionID, ‚Ä¶)` ‚Äî passing the parent session id as the first argument ‚Äî which spins up a **child session** with its own tools, system prompt, and context window, runs the subagent's prompt to completion, and returns the result to the parent's `task` tool part. Built-in subagents are `general`, `explore`, `scout`; users can also invoke one via `@<name>`. Foreground subagents block the parent conversation until they finish (per OpenCode's docs, which mirror Claude Code's foreground/background distinction). `capabilities.json` declares `subagents: true`. This matches the scenario's **subagents** primitive exactly: a child session, forked context, linked to the parent, holding the parent in `working` until it completes.

## daemon_capability: full

The daemon observes the full topology through opencode's `ProcessOwnedStore` (SQLite), with no per-adapter subagent counter required:

1. **Child session appears with `ParentSessionID = parent.session_id`.** OpenCode persists the parent link in the `session.parent_id` column (`parent_id text()` in opencode's `session.sql.ts`). The irrlicht watcher's session-table poll selects `id, directory, time_updated, parent_id` (`watcher.go:257,266,284`) and emits `EventNewSession` with `ParentSessionID: s.parentID` (`watcher.go:346`). The child's `directory` equals the parent's cwd, so it passes the watcher's `liveCWDs` gate (`watcher.go:330`) because a live opencode process owns that cwd. Covered by `TestScanSessions_ParentSessionID` (`watcher_test.go:330`).
2. **`parent_linked` lifecycle event.** The detector stores `ParentSessionID` on the new `SessionState` and records `lifecycle.KindParentLinked` (`session_detector_activity.go:88-110`) ‚Äî agent-agnostic, fired for any source that supplies `ParentSessionID`.
3. **`SubagentCount(parent) = N`.** `session.ComputeSubagentSummary` (`grouped.go:201-235`) derives the count from child `SessionState`s whose `ParentSessionID` matches the parent (the file/store-based component). opencode's in-process `OpenSubagents` is 0, so the count comes entirely from the linked-children graph ‚Äî exactly the same derivation claudecode uses.
4. **`SubagentCount` decreases as each child ends.** Each child session accrues its own `step-finish` parts; the watcher's `scanParts` (which queries by `session_id` for every tracked session, including children ‚Äî the session scan has no `parent_id IS NULL` filter) emits `EventActivity{Terminal:true}` for the child's terminal turn, transitioning it to ready.
5. **Parent: working ‚Üí ready only after the last child ends.** `reevaluateParent` (`session_detector_subagent.go:170-221`) holds the parent in `working` while `hasActiveChildren(parentID)` is true, and only re-classifies once the parent's own turn `IsAgentDone()` AND no child is still working/waiting.

All five user-observable signals land in irrlicht's existing opencode transport with no new daemon work. (The claudecode-specific `applySubagentCompletions`/`finishOrphanedChildren` filename-suffix matching at `session_detector_subagent.go:41-147` does NOT apply to opencode ‚Äî those key on the `<parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` JSONL layout. opencode's children are real `SessionState`s driven to ready by their own store-derived activity, so the generic `reevaluateParent` path suffices.)

## driver_capability: ready

A foreground-subagent recipe needs only `send` (a prompt that asks opencode to delegate to a subagent) + `wait_turn` + `sleep` ‚Äî all present in `scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`'s headless path (`opencode run --format default -- <text>`). No new step primitive (no `keys`/`sigkill`/`reset_session`) is required, so this is `ready`, not a `gap`.

The spawn proof does NOT depend on the driver exporting the child's transcript. The driver's headless export captures only the parent session (`WHERE p.session_id = $SESSION_ID`, with the parent selected via `parent_id IS NULL`), but `run-cell.sh` has an explicit opencode branch in its subagents gate (`run-cell.sh:447-450`): for opencode the **`parent_linked` event count in the daemon recording is the spawn proof** ‚Äî no separate child `.jsonl` file is required (unlike claudecode's `<parent>/subagents/` probe). The daemon records `parent_linked` directly from the live SQLite DB, so `subagent_transcripts_min: 1` is satisfied by the recorded `parent_linked` count.

## Why confidence 0.82 (not 0.9+)

The daemon and driver mechanics are documented in source and covered by a passing unit test, but the end-to-end behavior is not yet demonstrated by a recording for this cell, and one recipe-stage risk remains: the model must actually CHOOSE to invoke the Task tool from a single prompt (non-deterministic; a recipe should phrase the prompt to make delegation overwhelmingly likely, e.g. an explicit @-mention of a built-in subagent like `@explore`). That is recipe-authoring, not a daemon/driver limit.
caveats: The scenario's `verify.contains_tool_call: "Agent"` is the claudecode-era canonical name; opencode's tool part carries `tool = "task"` (lowercase). The daemon DOES emit the tool use (parser.go parseToolPart reads `raw["tool"]` generically), but the spec/expected mapping must assert `task` (or normalize task‚ÜíAgent) at the record/validate stage ‚Äî not a daemon or driver gap.

Subagent invocation is model-driven: a single prompt may not deterministically trigger the Task tool. The recipe should force delegation (explicit `@explore`/`@general` mention or a prompt that clearly fits a read-only explore subagent) so the run reliably spawns a child.

The subagent kind (`general`/`explore`/`scout`) is carried in the parent's `task` tool input but is not surfaced as a first-class dashboard attribute ‚Äî the count chip shows N, not which kind. Same narrow gap as claudecode; an enhancement, not a spec failure.

sources.note: Declares subagents: true ‚Äî the required-feature gate (primitive `subagents`) for this scenario.
Subagents run in separate child sessions; invoked automatically via the Task tool or manually via @mention; built-ins general/explore/scout; foreground subagents block the parent.
session table schema: `parent_id: text().$type<SessionID>()` ‚Äî the parent link the irrlicht watcher reads.
Task tool execute spins up a new session via Session.create(ctx.sessionID, ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî parent id passed as first arg, establishing the child's parent_id.

Session-table poll selects parent_id (lines 257,266,284) and emits EventNewSession with ParentSessionID (line 346); liveCWDs gate at 330; child parts flow through scanParts (no parent_id filter on the session scan).
TestScanSessions_ParentSessionID (line 330) proves the watcher emits EventNewSession with ParentSessionID set from the DB parent_id column.
Lines 88-110: detector stores ParentSessionID and records lifecycle.KindParentLinked ‚Äî agent-agnostic, fired for any source supplying ParentSessionID.

reevaluateParent (170-221) + hasActiveChildren hold the parent in working until the last child ends; the claudecode-specific filename-suffix matchers (41-147) do not apply to opencode's store-backed children.
ComputeSubagentSummary (201-235) derives SubagentCount from linked child SessionStates; opencode's in-process OpenSubagents is 0, so the count is purely graph-derived.
Lines 447-450: opencode subagents gate uses the daemon recording's parent_linked count as the spawn proof ‚Äî no separate child .jsonl file required (unlike the claudecode <parent>/subagents/ probe).
Headless path drives plain prompts via `opencode run -- <text>` (send/wait_turn/sleep); a subagent-triggering prompt needs no new step primitive ‚Üí driver ready.
Retired 2026-05-22 assessment (same yes/full/ready verdict); this reassessment re-derives and ratifies it from scratch after finding the live cell unbacked.

preconditions: opencode CLI on PATH and authenticated against its default model (run a single message in the opencode TUI to confirm; the dev env default is a local LM Studio model, so no remote key is needed). Verified opencode 1.14.50.
irrlichd is recording with the opencode SQLite watcher enabled ‚Äî either run-cell.sh in isolated/coexist mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
opencode's `task` (subagent) tool is available ‚Äî capabilities.json declares subagents:true; the built-in `general`/`explore`/`scout` subagents and `@<name>` invocation are on by default
setup: The interactive driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh) creates a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/ and launches a blocking `opencode run --format default -- "<prompt>"` for the single send (no --model flag, so opencode uses its configured default model); the run blocks until the parent assistant turn finishes ‚Äî including the time the parent spends awaiting the foreground subagent ‚Äî so wait_turn is a no-op
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic, tiny file to read and report back (keeps the child turn short and the recording cheap)

OpenCode session state lives in its SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db), not per-cwd transcript files. The parent invokes the `task` tool, which calls Session.create(parentSessionID, ‚Ä¶); the child session row carries `parent_id = parent.id`. The daemon's ProcessOwnedStore watcher polls the session table, surfaces the child as transcript_new, and the detector emits parent_linked. For opencode the daemon-recorded parent_linked count IS the spawn proof (run-cell.sh:447-450) ‚Äî NO separate child .jsonl file on disk is required (unlike claudecode's <parent>/subagents/ probe)

The default model is a small, slow local LM Studio model (~30‚Äì45s/turn), and a subagent run is two turns deep (parent + child), so the trailing sleep and timeout are generous: a slow parent+child sequence must not trip a false timeout, and the trailing sleep must outlast the parent's working‚Üíready settle after the child returns

script.text: Use the task tool to launch a subagent (the built-in 'explore' agent) that reads the README.md file in the current directory and reports its first heading. Wait for that subagent to finish, then reply with exactly the word: done.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent ses_‚Ä¶ session entering ready, then a single ready‚Üíworking transition driven by the user prompt

events.jsonl contains at least 1 parent_linked event whose parent_session_id is the parent ses_‚Ä¶ UUID ‚Äî the child session row (parent_id set) was discovered by the SQLite watcher and bound to the parent (this is opencode's spawn proof; no child .jsonl is expected on disk)
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the child session born after the parent turn started

no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent ses_‚Ä¶ between parent_linked and the final working‚Üíready (the parent's outstanding `task` tool part holds it in working until the child reports back)
the child session transitions working‚Üíready when its read/report work completes (dashboard subagent count drops 1 ‚Üí 0)
events.jsonl shows a working‚Üíready transition for the parent ses_‚Ä¶ at turn end, after the child finishes
final session state for the parent is ready

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Reasoning

The `foreground-subagent` scenario exercises the `subagents` primitive: a parent agent spawns one or more foreground child sessions via an Agent/Task tool, holds `working` until the last child completes, and the children are linked to the parent by `ParentSessionID` with a live `SubagentCount`. The scenario's `requires` are `[headless_mode, subagents]`.

pi has `headless_mode` (`--print`/`-p`/JSON mode) but explicitly **does not have a subagent primitive**. Three independent primary sources agree:

1. **capabilities.json** ‚Äî pi's `subagents` feature flag is `false` (the canonical capability key for the `subagents` primitive this scenario anchors on).
2. **Binary / dist scan (Step 2.5 guard)** ‚Äî `pi` resolves to a Node ESM launcher (`#!/usr/bin/env node` importing `./main.js` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.70.0/dist`). A keyword scan over the launcher, the entire `dist/` bundle, AND the README for `sub.?agent | subAgent | spawnAgent | launchAgent | "Task" | Task tool | childSession | delegate | parentSessionId` returns **zero implementation hits** ‚Äî the only matches anywhere are the README's explicit "not-built" disclaimers. There is no Agent/Task tool, no child-session spawn path, and no parent/child linkage anywhere in the shipped code.
3. **README (@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent 0.70.0)** ‚Äî states verbatim "No sub-agents. There's many ways to do this. Spawn pi instances via tmux, or build your own with extensions...", lists "Sub-agents and plan mode" under what extensions could add (i.e. not stock), and the intro: "Pi ships with powerful defaults but skips features like sub agents and plan mode."

Because stock pi cannot spawn a child agent session at all, there is no parent `working` arc wrapping a child lifetime, no `ParentSessionID` to bind, and no `SubagentCount` to report. The pi parser (`parser.go`) confirms there is nothing to observe even if one tried: it handles only `session`/`model_change`/`compaction`/`thinking_level_change` and `message` blocks (assistant/user/toolResult/bashExecution) ‚Äî there is no event kind, no child-transcript path under `<parent>/subagents/`, and no parent-linking that could emit `parent_linked`. pi's `FilesUnderRoot` source watches `~/.pi/agent/sessions/--<cwd>--/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl`, one flat transcript per pi instance, with no nested subagent transcript directory.

The verdict is frozen at the **agent layer**: the feature does not exist in stock pi, so `irrlicht_observes` is `n/a` (there is no behavior to observe, as opposed to a transport gap). This ratifies the prior matrix verdict (`no` / `n/a`) and the column reassessment.
caveats: A third-party pi package or TypeScript extension could add a subagent loop (e.g. spawning pi instances via tmux), but that is not stock pi and would not produce the canonical Agent/Task child-session shape (ParentSessionID linkage, <parent>/subagents/ transcript, SubagentCount) this scenario asserts.

Spawning sibling pi instances via tmux would surface as independent top-level FilesUnderRoot sessions, not parent-linked children ‚Äî so even that workaround would not satisfy the parent_linked / subagent_transcripts assertions.

sources.note: "subagents": false ‚Äî the capability key the foreground-subagent primitive anchors on
0.70.0 README: 'No sub-agents.' (L452); 'skips features like sub agents and plan mode' (L23); 'Sub-agents and plan mode' listed under extension-only possibilities (L343)
Parser handles only session/model_change/compaction/message(assistant,user,toolResult,bashExecution); no Agent/Task tool, no child-transcript, no parent_linked event ‚Äî nothing to observe
Source: FilesUnderRoot{~/.pi/agent/sessions} ‚Äî one flat transcript per pi instance; no nested <parent>/subagents/ path exists

sources.ref: strings $(which pi); grep -rliE 'sub.?agent|spawnAgent|launchAgent|"Task"|Task tool|childSession|parentSessionId' dist/ README.md
sources.note: Step 2.5 binary/dist scan over @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.70.0: zero implementation hits; only README 'not-built' disclaimers matched
